category_title: Seventh-grade urban students exhibiting off-task behaviors such as nonacademic conversations, hitting, or sleeping
minimal_context: The student is working on a mapping assignment using a map, notebook, and sharpened pencil in a seventh-grade social studies classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please take out your map, notebook, and a sharpened pencil to begin the mapping assignment.

STUDENT: See? My notebook is out, too. Am I doing a good job on my map?

TUTOR: Just keep working quietly.

STUDENT: (Stops working on the map) I don't like this. We need to go back to how she was doing class.

TUTOR: Don't talk out of turn. You need to stay in your seat and complete your work.

STUDENT: (Gets out of the seat and stands up, moving the chair) I'm not doing this! (Starts a nonacademic conversation with a peer across the room and makes inappropriate noises)

options.A: Design mapping tasks and send messages that highlight the value of engagement for pursuing deep learning and improvement of social studies knowledge and skills.
options.B: Provide the student with opportunities to use various learning styles or intelligences during the mapping assignments and other social studies activities.
options.C: Implement an intervention package consisting of reduced teacher reprimands and increased behavior-specific praise within a framework of student-constructed rules.
options.D: Implement a two-pocket folder system wherein the left side is dedicated to mapping assignments to be completed each day and the right side to completed work.
